content:
Hello, Rust4Linux newbie question: I've fixed signedness bug in firmware.rs that caused failure of cross-compilation on rust-dev branch.
Is it OK to create a PR on GH for this, or even simple patches need to take emailed patch routes?

I think the rust-dev branch is some months old now -- I think @Boqun Feng doesn't update it anymore. We should probably archive/remove it.

But, yeah, all patches need to appear in the mailing list, we don't use GitHub PRs.
My bad, let me at least sync it with rust-next for now.
No worries at all! If you want to have/use it we can keep it.
I need to think more on whether we should archive/remove it, but let me just make it sync with rust-next from now.
Start from marking this branch as obsolete on https://rust-for-linux.com/branches
